rawText:
Sr    Description                Quantity     UOM
1     Ball Bearing  6204ZZ Type    50           Pcs
2     Oil Seal 35mm                100          Pcs
3     Circlip External             200          Pcs

Total Quantity                     350
note: Header maps sr=col0, desc=col1, qty=col2. 'Ball Bearing  6204ZZ Type' has a 2-space gap, so the row splits to [1][Ball Bearing][6204ZZ Type][50][Pcs]; qty col2='6204ZZ Type' -> first numeric 6204. Reads qty 6204 and description 'Ball Bearing' instead of 50 / 'Ball Bearing 6204ZZ Type'. Other two rows parse fine.

rawText:
Description                   Qty     Unit
Copper Wire   Insulated       25      Roll
Cable Gland   Brass Body      40      Nos
Cable Tie 200mm Black          10      Pack

Grand Total                   75
note: desc=col0, qty=col1. Both 'Copper Wire   Insulated' and 'Cable Gland   Brass Body' have internal 2-space gaps, so their real qty is pushed to col2 while col1 holds a non-numeric fragment ('Insulated' / 'Brass Body'). Each row therefore has 4 columns but no positive number in the qty column -> not a data row, and with 3+ columns it is SKIPPED (not appended) -> both items vanish. Only 'Cable Tie 200mm Black' (single spaces) survives.

rawText:
Item Description                 Qty     Rate      Amount
MS Plate   500 x 250 mm          12      450.00    5400.00
Angle Iron 25mm                  30      120.00    3600.00
Round Bar 16mm                   18      95.00     1710.00

Sub Total                                          10710.00
note: No unit column (Qty/Rate/Amount). desc=col0, qty=col1. 'MS Plate   500 x 250 mm' splits on the 3-space gap, so col1='500 x 250 mm' -> first numeric 500. Reads qty 500 (real 12) and description just 'MS Plate'. The dimension inside a plate spec is a classic embedded-number trap.

rawText:
Description                        Qty      Unit
Laptop Charger   65W Type-C        40       Pcs
USB Cable 2m                       100      Pcs
Wireless Mouse   Model M185        25       Pcs

Total                              165
note: desc=col0, qty=col1. Two product names carry a tab-like 2-3 space gap before a spec fragment that begins with digits: 'Laptop Charger   65W Type-C' -> col1='65W Type-C' -> 65 (real 40); 'Wireless Mouse   Model M185' -> col1='Model M185' -> 185 (real 25). Both descriptions are truncated to the first fragment. USB Cable parses correctly.

rawText:
S.No   Particulars                 Ordered Qty    Unit
1      Ceramic Tile   Glossy White    500          Sqft
2      Grout Powder Grey              50           Kg
3      Tile Spacer   2mm Cross        20           Box

Net Total                            570
note: Synonym headers: Particulars (desc) + Ordered Qty (qty). Layout maps sno=col0, desc=col1, qty=col2. 'Ceramic Tile   Glossy White' splits so col2='Glossy White' (non-numeric) -> 5 cols, not a data row -> SKIPPED -> missing. 'Tile Spacer   2mm Cross' -> col2='2mm Cross' -> 2, so qty reads 2 instead of 20. Grout Powder parses correctly.

rawText:
Description                      Qty      Unit
Bolt M10   Grade 8               200      Pcs
Nut M10   Grade 8                200      Pcs
Washer M10   Flat Type           400      Pcs

Total Pcs                        800
note: desc=col0, qty=col1. Subtle attack: 'Bolt M10   Grade 8' and 'Nut M10   Grade 8' split so col1='Grade 8' -> qty reads 8 (a plausible-looking small quantity) instead of 200 for both. 'Washer M10   Flat Type' -> col1='Flat Type' (non-numeric) -> 4 cols, SKIPPED -> missing. All three real items are broken; the small wrong number is the nastiest because it looks legitimate.

2    Sodium Hydroxide Pellets        25      Kg
3    Acetone   99% Pure              5       Ltr

note: sr=col0, desc=col1, qty=col2. Chemical concentrations embedded in names: 'Hydrochloric Acid   32%' -> col2='32%' -> 32 (real 10); 'Acetone   99% Pure' -> col2='99% Pure' -> 99 (real 5). Both quantities badly wrong and descriptions truncated. Sodium Hydroxide (single spaces) parses correctly.

rawText:

   Heavy Duty   Powder Coated   Grey Finish
Workbench Steel Top                2       Set
   With Drawers   Lockable   Anti Rust
Storage Cabinet Metal              6       Nos

note: The main rows parse with correct qty, but each item's description wraps to a second physical line. Those wrap lines contain internal 2-space gaps ('Heavy Duty   Powder Coated   Grey Finish') so they split into 3+ columns. Continuation only appends lines that split into <=2 columns; a 3+ column non-data line is SKIPPED, so the wrapped detail is dropped and every description is truncated to its first line.

note: The first three lines are genuine tax-law reference books whose titles begin with CGST/SGST/IGST — exactly the tax-line footer markers. A loose matcher drops three real items; the real CGST/SGST footers sit below.

rawText:
PURCHASE ORDER                              No: PO-2214
Supplier: Fauji Cement Depot

Item Description               Qty      Rate      Amount
Portland Cement 50kg           200      850       170,000
Fine River Sand (per truck)             4,500     4,500
Steel Bars 12mm Grade 60       150      265       39,750

Grand Total                                       214,250
Amount in Words: Two Lac Fourteen Thousand Only
note: Blank Qty cell on the Sand row. After a 2+-space split it becomes [desc, 4,500, 4,500], so the Rate value slides into column index 1 (the Qty column) and is read as quantity 4500 instead of the real 1 truck.

rawText:
PURCHASE ORDER - MediCare Distributors
PO No: MC-7781

Product Description           Pack Qty    Order Qty    UOM
Paracetamol 500mg Tablets     10          500          Box
Amoxicillin 250mg Capsules    12          300          Box
ORS Sachets Orange            50          200          Box

Sub Total
note: Two columns both contain the qty synonym 'Qty'. Neither is an exact 'Qty' match, so containment wins and 'Pack Qty' (column index 1, the leftmost match) is chosen. The extractor reads the pack size (10/12/50) instead of the real ordered quantity (500/300/200).

rawText:
SUPPLY ORDER
M/s Al-Rehman Electricals

Nos    Material Description          Quantity    Unit
1      Copper Wire 4mm               250         Coil
2      PVC Conduit 20mm              400         Pcs
3      Junction Box 4x4              150         Pcs

Net Total
note: The leftmost serial-number column is headed 'Nos', which is a qty synonym and matches exactly at index 0 — the same as 'Quantity' at index 2. Scanning columns left-to-right, 'Nos' is hit first, so the extractor reads the serial numbers 1/2/3 as quantities instead of 250/400/150.

rawText:
PURCHASE ORDER
Buyer: Karachi Water Board

Item Description               Qty     UOM
Gate Valve 4"     Class 150    20      Nos
Ball Valve 2"     Class 300    35      Nos
Check Valve 6"    Class 150    12      Nos

Grand Total
note: The valve class rating ('Class 150') is separated from the description by 2+ spaces, so it becomes its own cell at column index 1 (the Qty column). The extractor reads the first numeric token of 'Class 150' as quantity 150 instead of the real 20, and the description loses its class suffix.

rawText:
PURCHASE ORDER - Site Materials
Project: Gulberg Tower

Sr   Description                       Qty    Unit
1    Cement OPC Bags                    500    Bag
2    Washed Sand - 3 truck loads              Load
3    Crush Aggregate 20mm              200    CFT

Sub Total
note: The Sand line's Qty cell is blank (quantity is stated inside the description, '3 truck loads'). The row splits into 3 cells and the Qty column index holds 'Load' (no number), so it is not a data row; with 3+ columns it is skipped, not appended. The item is dropped entirely.

rawText:
PURCHASE ORDER - Wellness Pharma
PO: WP-3390

Product                       Qty    Free    Unit
Cough Syrup 100ml             100    10      Btl
Multivitamin Tablets          200    20      Strip
Scheme: Buy 10 Get 1 Free     10     1       Offer

Grand Total
note: The pharma scheme/offer line is not an ordered item, but it splits into 4 cells with a positive number (10) at the Qty column, so the extractor treats it as a genuine data row and emits a phantom item.

rawText:
PURCHASE ORDER - Referenz 88231
Lieferant: Hamburg Fasteners GmbH

Material Description           Quantity     Unit
Hex Bolt M8 Galvanized         10.000       Pcs
Flat Washer M8 Stainless       25.000       Pcs
Hex Nut M8 Zinc Plated         5.500        Pcs

Gesamt / Total
note: European supplier uses '.' as the thousands separator. The extractor treats '.' as a decimal point, so '10.000' parses to 10 and '5.500' to 5.5, instead of the real 10000 and 5500.

rawText:
PURCHASE ORDER - Shree Constructions Pvt Ltd
PO No: SC/2026/114

Description                   Quantity     UOM
Portland Cement OPC 43         1,00,000     Kg
Steel TMT Bars Fe500           2,50,000     Kg
River Sand Washed                50,000     Cft

Grand Total
note: Indian lakh grouping ('1,00,000'). A Western thousands-aware numeric regex (\d{1,3}(,\d{3})*) fails on the ',00,' group and captures only the leading '1'/'2', so the extractor reads 1 and 2 instead of 100000 and 250000. The standard-grouped '50,000' parses fine, masking the bug.

rawText:
PURCHASE ORDER - Al-Noor Tiles
PO: ANT-556

Item Description              Qty        Unit
Vitrified Floor Tiles 60x60   10 x 12    Box
Ceramic Wall Tiles 30x60      8 x 12     Box
Skirting Tiles 10x60          15         Box

Sub Total
note: Quantity written as cartons x boxes-per-carton ('10 x 12'). Single spaces keep it in one cell, and 'first numeric token' captures only 10/8, dropping the multiplier. Real order total is 120/96 boxes. The plain '15' row parses correctly, hiding the pattern.

rawText:
PURCHASE ORDER - Stride Footwear
PO: SF-2201

Item Description              Qty      Unit
Men Formal Shoes Black    10-12    240      Pair
Men Casual Shoes Brown    8-10     180      Pair
Ladies Sandals Tan        6-8      150      Pair

Grand Total
note: An unlabelled size-range column ('10-12') sits between the description and the true quantity, and the header never accounts for it. Each row gains an extra cell, so the Qty column index holds the range; 'first numeric token' reads 10/8/6 instead of 240/180/150, and the real qty spills into the Unit column.

rawText:
PURCHASE ORDER - Office Furniture
PO: OF-889

Sr   Description                  Rate      Units
1    Executive Desk Walnut        45,000    10
2    Visitor Chair Leather x15    12,000
3    Bookshelf Oak 5-Tier         22,000    8

Grand Total
note: The quantity column ('Units') is the last column. The Visitor Chair row omits it (qty is embedded as 'x15' in the description), so the row has too few cells to reach the Units column index and is skipped. The item is lost even though its quantity (15) is present in the text.

rawText:
PURCHASE ORDER - MegaMart Retail
PO: MM-4471

Item Description                Qty     Unit
LED TV Samsung 55"    1200 x 700   30      Nos
Refrigerator LG 260L  600 x 650    18      Nos
Microwave Oven 25L               45      Nos

Grand Total
note: Product carton dimensions ('1200 x 700') are wide-spaced off the description on some rows, forming an extra cell that lands in the Qty column; the extractor reads 1200/600 instead of 30/18. The Microwave row has no dimension, so its 45 parses correctly, showing the misalignment is row-dependent.
